strategic_profile.executive_summary: The University of Central Florida (UCF) is a massive, public R1 research university located in Orlando, Florida. Known as one of the largest universities in the United States, it serves as a major talent pipeline for the aerospace, defense, and hospitality industries.
strategic_profile.market_position: Large Public Research University (R1) / Hispanic-Serving Institution

analyst_takeaways.insight: UCF is highly regarded for producing top-tier tech and engineering talent, functioning as a primary feeder for aerospace and defense companies like Lockheed Martin.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students looking for career-focused STEM programs with robust internship pipelines will find significant value here.

analyst_takeaways.insight: While traditionally accessible, UCF has become increasingly selective with a 40% acceptance rate and an admitted student weighted GPA average of 4.18.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants can no longer treat UCF as a safe fallback; it requires strong academic rigor and solid standardized test scores.

analyst_takeaways.insight: With in-state tuition frozen around $6,368 and 70% of first-time-in-college students graduating without educational debt, UCF offers immense ROI.
analyst_takeaways.implication: In-state students have access to a top-tier research education at an exceptionally low cost.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: A highly diverse, pedestrian-oriented campus structured in concentric circles, featuring a lively social scene, strong Greek life, and Division I Big 12 athletics.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Primarily a commuter-heavy campus situated 13 miles east of downtown Orlando, with significant traffic and limited parking commonly cited as challenges by students.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: Very high research activity (R1), particularly in optics, modeling and simulation, and space technologies, located adjacent to the Central Florida Research Park.

current_status.test_policy_details: Standardized test scores (SAT or ACT) are required for admission per Florida Board of Governors regulations.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist admit rate calculated based on the number of students who accepted a spot on the waitlist.

gpa.notes: UCF recalculates high school GPA based on core academic courses and foreign language.

colleges.name: College of Engineering and Computer Science
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students aiming for careers in aerospace, defense, or high-tech software engineering.
colleges.housing_profile: Standard university housing or off-campus apartments.

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.special_requirements: Progress Policy: Students will be excluded from the major if they accumulate 10 or more unsuccessful attempts across all UCF courses, or fail to earn a 'C' or better in the same UCF course by the third attempt.

majors.name: Information Technology
majors.special_requirements: Progress Policy: Subject to the same exclusion policies as Computer Science for accumulating excessive unsuccessful course attempts.

colleges.name: College of Community Innovation and Education
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Best for students seeking public service, education, or criminal justice careers.

majors.name: Criminal Justice
majors.special_requirements: Requires a minimum 2.5 overall GPA and a 'C' or better in all core courses to progress in the program.

application_deadlines.notes: UCF operates on a Rolling Admissions model. Applying early (Oct-Nov) is highly encouraged.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: The Top 10 Knights program guarantees admission to Florida high school students in the top 10% of their class who meet specific minimum test scores (e.g., 1090 SAT).

major_selection_tactics: Apply early in the rolling admissions cycle, as programs can reach capacity.
Students uncertain about STEM requirements may apply Undeclared and utilize UCF's Major Exploration services.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: UCF allows students to apply undeclared, but transferring into capped majors like those in Engineering and Computer Science requires adhering to strict academic progress policies later on.

financials.tuition_model: Per credit hour for part-time, flat rate for full-time standard load.

ap_policy.general_rule: UCF awards credit for AP scores of 3, 4, or 5.
exceptions: Maximum of 45 credit hours combined can be accepted via AP, IB, CLEP, and other examination credits.
ap_policy.usage: Credits can be used to bypass introductory prerequisites and satisfy general education requirements.

ib_policy.general_rule: Maximum of 30 hours of credit granted for scores of 4 or higher.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: DirectConnect to UCF guarantees admission to students who complete their associate degree at participating local Florida colleges.

what_it_takes: A high recalculated core GPA (averaging 4.18) with a rigorous AP/IB course load.
A solid SAT/ACT score to remain competitive, especially for out-of-state students or competitive STEM tracks.
common_activities: Involvement in robotics or coding (for CECS applicants).
Community service and leadership in clubs.
essay_tips: Focus on resilience, leadership, and a clear connection to UCF's culture of innovation.
red_flags: Poor standardized test scores with no mitigating circumstances.
insights: UCF is widely praised for its internship networks with major local employers like Disney, Lockheed Martin, and AdventHealth.
The massive student body means students must be proactive to secure advising and register for classes on time.
